sections.title: 109 會 考 補考 (32~35)
sections.content:
Here is a page of this month's Lenses Magazine.
@ Meet Our Conir ib OP
EDWARD FORD has been writing for us since 2008. Stories about his
visits abroad are most popular with our readers. This month he writes
5] about his vacation in an old castle and how scared he was during his stay
there (p.34). Ford now lives in Lemon City, with his two dogs.
HANNAH GIBSON believes “a good picture says alot more than words.” SE
This month her photos tell us the history of a six-hundred-year-old church >
on Orange Island (p.96). Gibson often flies between Lemon City and Apple
Town. d KN
ALBERT LANCE started writing for TV shows in 2000 and today is
© one of the best-selling writers in the country. This month, he interviews
Marianne Cruz about how she left the business world and became a
world-famous dancer (p.102).
BRETT SHAW is known for drawing for City Daily, Apple Town's
biggest newspaper. His pictures can be seen at www.brettshawdrawing.
net. This month he draws for Ford's trip (p.34). Shaw's first picture
book Catching Watermeions comes out this month.
MAGGIE WALSH writes about movies in every Friday's City Daily.
全 Her writings can often decide how many movie tickets will be sold. This
month, she tells us what makes a good action movie (p.28). Walsh also
teaches at Apple Town Art School.
( )32, Which story is NOT covered (3) in this month's Lenses Magazine?
(A) Someone's life changes.
(B) The past of an old church.
(©) A trip to a foreign country.
(D) The way to start a newspaper.
( )33. What do we know about these five people?
(A) One was a popular TV show actor.
(B) One has been famous for writing children’s books.
(C) One's ideas about movies are important for those who see movies.
(D) One's writings started to appear in Lenses Magazine 20 years ago.
( )34, What does contributors mean in the reading?
(A) People who write, draw pictures, or take photos for a magazine.
(B) People who decide where in a magazine to put writings, pictures or photos.
(C) People who write letters to talk about a magazine's writings, pictures or photos.
(D) People who look for a job of writing, drawing pictures, or taking photos for a magazine.
( )35, Which is most likely (可 能 ) Brett Shaw s drawing in this month's Lenses Magazine?

sections.title: 110 會 考 補考 (20~22)
sections.content:
人
(At Dora’s house)
Jason : How was your trip to France?
Dora : Wonderful! I visited the Louvre, one of the world’s largest museums.
Jason : Did you know there's another Louvre in the city of Lens, just two hours by car from Paris?
Dora : Yes, but I just stayedin Paris. Maybe next time.
Jason : I see. So are these the photos you took in France?
Dora : Right. I’m picking a photo for my foreign pen friend, but I just can't decide which is the
best.
Jason : Let me see... How about this one?
Dora : Why do you like it?
Jason : Don’t you think you look more beautiful in it? We all look better with our left side facing
the camera.
Dora : How so?
Jason : A book I just read says that the left side of our faces works harder than the right side when
we cry or laugh. That's why it is always our best side.
Dora : And that’s why Leonardo da Vinci painted Mona Lisa that way!
)
( ) 20, What do we know about Dora?
(A) She went to France with Jason.
(B) She visited the Louvre in Lens.
(C) She is not good at taking pictures.
(D) She had trouble deciding which photo to choose.
( )21. Which is most likely (186) said in the book that Jason read?
(A) Our faces work to show how we feel.
(B) We need a good camera to take nice pictures.
(C) It is good for your health to sleep on your side.
(D) It takes practice to paint like Leonardo da Vinci.
( ) 22, What does this one most likely look like?

sections.content:
(在 Dora 家 )
Jason : 妳 的 法 國 之 旅 恕 何 ?
Dora: 太 棒 了 ! 我 參觀 了 羅浮 宮 , 世 界 上 最 大 的 博物 館 之 一 。
Jason : 妳 知道 Lens 市 還 有 另 一 座 羅 浮 宮 , 離 巴黎 只 要 兩 個 小 時 的 車 程 嗎 ?
Dora : RAE (HR RATERS ° that PRUE +
Jason : 了 解 。 所 以 這 些 是 妳 在 法 國 拍 的 照片 嗎 ?
Dora: 對 啊 。 我 正在 挑 一 張 照片 給 我 的 外 國 筆友 , 但 我 就 是 沒 辦法 決定 哪 張 最 好 看 。
Jason: 讓 我 看 看 ......。 這 張 妃 何 ?
Dora: 你 為 什麼 喜歡 它 ?
Jason : 妳 不 覺得 妳 這 張 看 起 來 比 較 涯 亮 嗎 ? 我 們 左 臉 面向 鏡頭 的 時 候 比 較 好 看 。
Dora : 怎麼 會 ?
Jason : 我 剛 讀 的 一 本 書 說 當 我 們 哭 或 笑 的 時 候 , 我 們 的 左 臉 會 比 右 臉 更 明顯 。 這 就 是 為 什麼 左 臉 總
是 我 們 最 好 看 的 一 邊 。
Dora: MEME ATES HRS tree eT |}
